resources.string: We will surface your listening habits once we know you better.
Total listening captured in the selected range.
How many sessions you completed per segment.
Average listening length for each segment.
Press play to start building your listening timeline
Split into 4-hour windows to reveal your daily rhythm.
Daily bars make week-to-week habits easy to compare.
Weekly bars show how the month is trending.
Monthly bars show seasonality across the year.
Yearly bars summarize your full history.
Compare how you listen across genres, artists, albums, and songs.
Press play to surface your listening highlights
Keep listening and your favorite artists will show up here.
Albums you revisit often will appear here.
Most played tracks for the selected time range.
Listen to your favorites to see them highlighted here.
How your listening time is distributed across your top tracks.
Play more tracks to see how focused your listening is.
Top 3 tracks account for %1$d%% of your listening time.
No compressed format reported hardware offload support.
No output routes were reported by Android.
Your indexed tracks match the decoders Android reports on this device.
The library reaches %1$d Hz, above the current output sample rate.
A full library rescan can fill missing MIME, bitrate, and sample-rate data.
Detect feat., ft., with in song titles
Show collaboration albums under main artist

PixelPlayer splits artist tags using character delimiters (/, ;, &) and word delimiters (feat., ft., vs., x). Word delimiters are matched case-insensitively.
"Extract Artists from Title" detects patterns like (feat. Artist) in song titles.
Backslash (\) can be used to escape character delimiters.
Artist settings have changed. Rescan your library to apply.
Stream music from your cloud accounts
This sort keeps its original order
Everything is allowed by default. Tap a folder to mark it as excluded from scans.
The Play Store link will be enabled from GitHub config.
PixelPlayer is now available on Google Play
Use the stable channel on Google Play for release updates while we keep beta builds active.
You\'re using a beta build that may contain bugs, crashes, or experimental features. Help us improve by reporting issues.
Bugs, crashes, or incomplete features may occur unexpectedly.
Some features may change or be removed without notice.
Beta builds may be more unstable than release versions.
Always check for updates before reporting a known issue.
What beta builds can change, break, or improve while testing.
Search first, then open a focused report for bugs, crashes, requests, or questions.
Share steps to reproduce, expected results, actual results, and your device/OS details.
A quick checklist before opening a new issue.
Search existing open and closed issues to avoid duplicates.
Update to the latest PixelPlayer version and confirm the problem still happens.
Restart the app and confirm the problem persists.
Try to reproduce it and write down the exact steps.
Feature request: Add a new feature or improvement.
Question: Use Discussions if enabled, or open an issue with a question label.
Copy these fields when something behaves incorrectly or crashes.
How often does it happen? Always / Sometimes / Rarely.
Install source: GitHub Release, debug build, nightly build, etc.
Extra context: SD card usage, special settings, permissions, etc.
Copy these fields when you want a new feature or improvement.
Problem statement: What problem are you trying to solve?
Proposed solution: How should it work?
Scope: Which screens or flows are affected?
Mockup or reference image if available.
Make the report easy to triage and safe to share.
Equalizer: Indicator shifts when switching preset tabs
Search: History list does not appear for empty query
Feature: Add \"Recently Added\" playlist sort option
Multiple unrelated problems in one issue.
Unredacted logs or screenshots with private data.
Before posting logs, screenshots, or videos, remove personal or private information.
How nightlies differ from releases, and what to include when they break.
Nightly builds are generated from the latest commit and may contain unfinished changes, temporary bugs, or regressions. They are more experimental than official releases.
Access them from the repository\'s GitHub Actions workflow artifacts if available.

When reporting an issue from a nightly build, always mention that it happened on a nightly build, not on the official release. Include the build date, workflow run name or number, or commit SHA if possible. Also check if the same problem happens on the latest official release.
If you\'re coming from beta 0.5.0, this update may need fresh library data instead of older cached state.
If metadata or library entries look wrong
Wrong song metadata, mismatched artists or albums, or duplicate-looking entries usually mean a clean install is the fix.
Queue + play respects your selection order.
Allow PixelPlayer to see your nearby devices and current Wi‑Fi so we can keep your cast, Bluetooth audio, and speakers in sync.
Needed to read and control your connected Bluetooth audio gear.
Android requires Location to share the Wi‑Fi network (SSID) you\'re on so we can find compatible cast devices.
We only use these permissions for device interconnectivity — casting, controlling nearby speakers, and keeping audio in sync.
Required to detect and control connected Bluetooth audio devices.
Make sure your TV or speaker is on and sharing the same Wi‑Fi network.
Turn on Wi‑Fi or Bluetooth to discover nearby devices

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_060_whats_new
string-array.item: Android Auto support is now available for in-car playback.
Wear OS support is live, including better watch-to-phone playback controls.
Cloud integrations expanded with Telegram, NetEase, QQ Music, and Google Drive improvements.
Recently Played and persistent queue restoration keep your listening session ready.
Backup & Restore v3 and account management tools are now included.
Lyrics got smarter with manual search fallback and storage improvements.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_060_improvements
string-array.item: Big performance pass across startup, library, queue, and player interactions.
Player, Cast, Lyrics, Artist, and Genre surfaces were redesigned for smoother use.
Navigation and search flows are more reliable, with safer route handling.
Audio playback compatibility improved for more devices and formats.
Multi-selection workflows were expanded across songs, albums, and playlists.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_060_fixes
string-array.item: Queue and shuffle behavior is now more stable and predictable.
Several background playback and casting edge cases were fixed.
Sleep Timer, Files tab navigation, and album artist crash issues were fixed.
Widget loading and service stability were improved to reduce overheating/memory issues.
General bug fixes and UI polish across the app.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_050_highlights
string-array.item: Material 3 Expressive UI Update
10-band Equalizer & Effects
New Library Sync Flow
AI Integration (Gemini Models)
M3U Playlist Import/Export
Deezer Artist Artwork Integration
Custom Playlist Covers

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_050_improvements
string-array.item: Settings Architecture Refactor
Queue & Player Animations
Baseline Profiles & Performance
Better Lyrics System with Sync Offset

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_050_fixes
string-array.item: Casting Stability Improvements
Player Sheet Stability
General Bug Fixes & Cleanup

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_040_highlights
string-array.item: Major navigation redesign
New file explorer for choosing source directories
New Connectivity and casting functionalities
Seamless continuity between remote devices
Gapless transition between songs
Crossfade control
New Custom Transitions feature (only for playlists)
Keep playing after closed the app
UI Optimizations
Improved stats feature
Redesigned Queue control with more features
Improved different filetypes support for playing and metadata editing
Improved permission controller
Minor bug fixes

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_030_whats_new
string-array.item: Introduced a richer listening stats hub with deeper insights into your sessions.
Launched a floating quick player to instantly open and preview local files.
Added a folders tab with a tree-style navigator and playlist-ready view.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_030_improvements
string-array.item: Refined the overall Material 3 UI for a cleaner and more cohesive experience.
Metadata editing now supports cover art change.
Smoothed out animations and transitions across the app for more fluid navigation.
Enhanced the artist screen layout with richer details and polish.
Upgraded DailyMix and YourMix generation with smarter, more diverse selections.
Strengthened the AI playlist generation.
Improved search relevance and presentation for faster discovery.
Expanded support for a broader range of audio file formats.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_030_fixes
string-array.item: Resolved metadata quirks so song details stay accurate everywhere.
Restored notification shortcuts so they reliably jump back into playback.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_020_added
string-array.item: Chromecast support for casting audio from your device.
In-app changelog to keep you updated on the latest features.
Support for .LRC files, both embedded and external.
Offline lyrics support.
Synchronized lyrics (synced with the song).
New screen to view the full queue.
Reorder and remove songs from the queue.
Mini-player gestures (swipe down to close).
Added more material animations.
New settings to customize the look and feel.
New settings to clear the cache.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_020_changed
string-array.item: Complete redesign of the user interface.
Complete redesign of the player.
Performance improvements in the library.
Improved application startup speed.
The AI now provides better results.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_020_fixed
string-array.item: Fixed various bugs in the tag editor.
Fixed a bug where the playback notification was not clearing.
Fixed several bugs that caused the app to crash.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_070_whats_new
string-array.item: Wear OS: Music transfer, local playback, queue sync, and remote control from watch.
AI: Groq AI and OpenRouter (experimental) integration with token optimization.
Cloud: Jellyfin support added.
Lyrics: Synced translation with dedicated toggle, Kugou LRC format support, text alignment customization, and improved remote loading.
UI/UX: Compact navigation bar mode, dynamic themes from album art palette, marquee for long titles, and new sorting options.
Telegram: Native topics support and enhanced display modes.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_070_improvements
string-array.item: Audio Engine: Complete overhaul with support for more formats (MIDI, ALAC, M4A) and decoder optimization.
Efficiency: Drastic energy consumption reduction, overheating fixes, and background task optimization (SyncWorker).
Database: Massive query optimizations and redesigned cover art cache to prevent data loss.
Startup: Improved loading time via Baseline Profile optimization.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_070_fixes
string-array.item: Playback: Fixed stuttering in Opus/MP3, ReplayGain errors during crossfades, and startup issues on Samsung decoders.
Stability: Eliminated crashes on startup, artist navigation, and Android 12+ devices.
UI: Fixed cover art flickering, text overflow in non-Latin scripts, and navigation bar/miniplayer behavior.
Security: Hardened credential handling, storage permissions, and media server communication.

string-array.name: presentation_batch_g_changelog_070_added
string-array.item: Spanish
French
Russian
Simplified Chinese
Indonesian
Italian
